evidence: Added benchmarks/chromux-benchmark.mjs and benchmark artifacts; V4 pass with benchmark.json, benchmark.png, run receipt, batch JSONL; V1/V2/V5 pass.
Extended run helpers with waitFor/assertPage and added runner snippets page-extract/form-flow/network-errors/page-assert; help/docs updated; V2 test.sh and V3 runtime smoke pass.
Upgraded cmdBatch with bounded retries, host backoff, p50/p95 summary, retry counts, host state, and failureKind taxonomy; V2 test.sh and V4 benchmark batch artifacts pass.
Added run waitFor/assertPage readiness proof with timeout diagnostics and current URL/title context; V2 validates waitFor/assertPage and V3 runtime smoke validates bundled form flow.
Implemented run --receipt with safe local artifact path handling and redaction summaries; V2 validates no inline code or sensitive strings leak and V3/V4 receipt artifacts are recorded.
Added ps --json profile diagnostics plus health gate queue depth and resource telemetry; V2 validates ps JSON and resource guard behavior; V3/V4 benchmark resources capture running profile state.
Added page-extract, form-flow, network-errors, and page-assert snippets; updated README/install/skills and CI snippet compilation; V2/V5 validate snippet contracts and docs.
Updated help, docs, skills, CI, test.sh, package allowlist, and package version 0.11.0; V1 npm pack dry-run, V2 test suite, V5 doc check pass; V6 remains for PR delivery step.
V4 benchmark.json records coldLaunchMs, warm ps, open, run extraction, full and interactive snapshots, interaction flow, and batch p50/p95; V4 passed with benchmark artifacts.
benchmarks/chromux-benchmark.mjs owns a deterministic localhost fixture server for pass/fail gates; V3 smoke and V4 full benchmark both passed without external pages.
Bundled form-flow runner executed a multi-step real Chrome flow through one run invocation and returned result data plus readiness proof; V3/V4 run receipt artifacts recorded.
cmdBatch now emits bounded worker results with workerId, session, duration, attempts, retryable, failureKind, host, queue telemetry, and p50/p95 summary; V2 and V4 passed.

Batch/resource guard paths classify timeout, resource_guard, queue_full, session_unresponsive, navigation, http_or_page, and unknown failures with host backoff and daemon health gate data; V2/V3/V4 passed.
run helpers include waitFor text, selector, expression, settled, quiet plus waitLoad and assertPage; V2 validates text waits and assertions against local fixtures.
waitFor/assertPage failures include waited condition, timeout budget, current URL/title, and last redacted observed value or error context; V2 failure diagnostics tests passed.
run --receipt writes chromux.run-receipt.v1 artifacts with codeStored:false and redacted strings/secrets; V2 validates inline code and typed text do not leak, with V3/V4 receipt artifacts recorded.
ps --json and daemon /health expose running profiles, paused state, stale daemon count, queue depth, queue limit, active gate, and resource guard state; V2/V3/V4 passed.

CLI-readable ps JSON, batch summaries, activity sanitization, and benchmark resource artifacts expose recent durations, failure counts, retry/resource guard information, and profile state; V2/V3/V4 passed.
Built-in runner snippets now cover page extraction/infinite scroll, form flow with readiness, network-error diagnostics, and page assertion patterns under snippets/_builtin/.
All new snippets compile with the runner helper signature in test.sh and CI, are exercised by local fixture benchmark flows, and are documented as runner material rather than top-level commands.

node chromux.mjs help remains the public command source of truth, documents run --receipt, batch retry/backoff, ps --json, and snippets, while deprecated eval stays off the primary help surface; V1/V5 passed.
./test.sh passed 118 checks covering scheduler, readiness, receipt redaction, snippet contracts, ps diagnostics, batch retry telemetry, benchmark smoke, and doc consistency.
npm pack --dry-run passed for @team-attention/chromux@0.11.0 with package allowlist contents only; planning artifacts are outside the published package.

PR delivery is approved and configured for prd/chromux-dominant-browser-automation; prd-ship refused status before receipt as expected, so PR URL and CI proof are deferred to the post-receipt ship step without staging unrelated files.

Optional delivery/CI check is blocked until receipt.json exists because prd-ship requires a complete prd-implement receipt before status/ship; user explicitly requested ship in this run, so delivery proof will be collected after finalization.
Benchmark harness and baseline evidence are implemented and verified by V1, V2, V4, V5 plus benchmark artifacts.
Fast-path run helpers and bundled snippets are implemented and verified by V1, V2, V3, V4, V5.
Batch scheduler retry/backoff/telemetry upgrades are implemented and verified by V2, V3, V4.
Readiness and action evidence helpers are implemented and verified by V2, V3, V4, V5.
Structured receipt and privacy-preserving redaction artifacts are implemented and verified by V1, V2, V3, V5.
Profile/resource diagnosis through ps --json and health gate telemetry is implemented and verified by V2, V3, V4.
Built-in snippets and agent-facing guidance are implemented, compiled, and documented; V1, V2, V3, V4, V5 passed.
Verification, docs, package version/allowlist, and PR delivery readiness are complete; V1, V2, V5 passed and V6 is deferred to prd-ship after receipt.

review.summary: PASS: PRD intent, non-goals, R/AC coverage, required verification artifacts, privacy guardrails, and delivery sequencing align; optional V6 delivery proof is deferred to prd-ship after complete receipt.
